economic_notes: First Nation has internally restricted capital bylaw funds split into Infrastructure, Capital, and Economic Development funds totalling $4,862,152. GBEs include TTQ Economic Development Corporation (100%), Takem Ti Qwelsa7 Eco-Resource Corporation (100%), Xa'xtsa Gas Station Ltd. (100%), 1020376 B.C. Ltd (33.33%), Tsetspa7 Forestry Limited Partnership (16.67%), and Tsetspa7 Loakam (Logging) Ltd. GBE financial information not available and excluded from consolidation. COVID-19 noted as subsequent event.
notes: Audit is qualified on two bases: (1) certain funded tangible capital assets including roads, bridges, buildings, and utility systems not capitalized in prior years; (2) GBE/GBP financial information not available and excluded from consolidation. Significant prior period restatement affecting 2019 comparatives for portfolio investments, accounts receivable, loans receivable, trust funds, replacement reserve, tangible capital assets, and various revenue/expense lines. St'at'imc (PC) 2011 Trust excluded as First Nation has no control. Long-term debt total of $3,419,675 includes demand loans ($701,805), long-term debt ($184,181), CMHC mortgages ($2,471,687), and capital lease ($62,603). Annual debt service calculated as principal repayments: long-term debt $73,431 + CMHC $112,597 + demand loan $42,598 + capital lease $7,365 = $235,991. total_government_transfers includes ISC $1,746,745 + Province of BC $1,267,695 + CMHC $140,735 + FNHA $38,649 + FNESC $163,574 + First Nation organizations $97,841 (partial) = ~$3,936,766 estimated. GBE investments not consolidated per modified equity method with no income recorded for current year.
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
